wave_interference_tank: The image shows a two-source ripple-tank interference diagram with circular crest and trough wavefronts.
The diagram shows two labeled wave sources and their circular interference wavefronts.
This figure shows a ripple-tank wave-interference pattern from two labeled sources.
The visual shows two wave sources with visible circular wavefront spacing.
The ripple-tank diagram shows two sources and a visible wave-interference pattern.

interference_point_choice_query: Using the source phase and wavefronts, choose the point with {target_condition_phrase}.
Among points A-E, which one matches {target_condition_phrase}?
Compare the two source phases at each candidate. Which point gives {target_condition_phrase}?
Select the labeled candidate point where the wavefronts produce {target_condition_phrase}.
path_difference_value_query: What is the absolute path difference from S1 and S2 to P, counted in lambda/2 steps?
Using the wavefront spacing, determine |S1P-S2P| in lambda/2 steps.
Read the two labeled guided paths to P. What integer path difference do they show in lambda/2 steps?
How many lambda/2 steps apart are the two source-to-P path lengths?
Count the ring-step difference between S1P and S2P. What is the absolute value?

task:interference_point_choice_query.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to one pixel point [x,y] at the center of the labeled candidate point matching the requested interference condition
task:interference_point_choice_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the matching candidate point letter as a string

task:path_difference_value_query.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of two source-to-P pixel segments; each segment is [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]], where each endpoint is a [x, y] pixel point, and endpoint order does not matter
task:path_difference_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the integer absolute path difference counted in lambda/2 steps

target_condition_phrase.description: The requested constructive or destructive interference condition.
